mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor demand that compresses consumer spending expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal consumption expenditures indicates a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that triggers equity risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60d returns in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where housing starts exceed 1.2 million units, rising construction activity amplifies corporate earnings growth expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts above 1.2 million units consistently coincide with negative or flat 20d forward returns across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly reflects expanding business demand, which sustains equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months consistently yield negative or zero 5d forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.5% annualized, it signals economic overheating that forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI acceleration above 2.5% fails to precede negative 20d equity returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll growth exceeds 200,000 while industrial production stagnates indicates a services-led demand shift that reduces manufacturing earnings drag, lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The specified payroll-industrial production divergence consistently fails to predict positive 60d returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets where the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, rising wage pressures compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Unemployment rates below 4.0% consistently coincide with positive or flat 20d forward returns.
